title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Code Recall
summary: A delayed publication review reconstructs semantic html rendering, contextual escaping, and determinism from revision, configuration, source bytes, document nodes, route manifest, rendered HTML, asset graph, validation report, artifact digest, deployment, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugg…
objectives: Render each validated node through one owned semantic mapping without reparsing generated strings as source.
Encode untrusted text and attribute values for their exact HTML context and reject unsafe script, style, comment, and URL placements.
Keep trusted renderer output distinct from untrusted author text and make every conversion into trusted markup explicit and reviewable.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

For Static-site publishing and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Render Determinism, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Python preserves insertion order, so all rendered output is reproducible.

stimulus.title: Render Determinism case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review publishing team handles case 3060 during an offline-first field documentation build. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Python preserves insertion order, so all rendered output is reproducible.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds. Artifact under review: deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer.

options.text: The Render Determinism record says deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Render Determinism.

For separate evidence from confidence, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Render Determinism for deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer.
The record names the assumption, applies Render Determinism, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Render Determinism: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review publishing team handles case 4679 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

steps.why: Retrieving Render Determinism through the concrete deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Record what required effort · Review Render Determinism, keep Render Determinism connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.
